items.title: Splunk Enterprise Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
items.description: Splunk Enterprise contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated user to create or truncate arbitrary files through a PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 88% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.8.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for Splunk Enterprise per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-06-21)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 88% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached.

impact.affected_versions: Splunk Enterprise (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all Splunk Enterprise installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.

items.title: Fortinet FortiSandbox OS Command Injection Vulnerability
items.description: Fortinet FortiSandbox contains an OS command injection vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via crafted HTTP requests.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 84% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.8.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for Fortinet FortiSandbox per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-07-19)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 84% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached; edge/network devices are internet-facing and a top initial-access vector.

impact.affected_versions: Fortinet FortiSandbox (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Identify all internet-facing Fortinet FortiSandbox devices. Check firmware versions against the advisory, and review access logs for unauthorized access, unusual processes, or configuration changes.

items.title: Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability
items.description: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Cisco Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME) contain a server-side request forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to write files to the underlying operating system that could be used later to elevate to root.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 42% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 8.6.

items.title: SimpleHelp Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
items.description: SimpleHelp contains an authentication bypass vulnerability in the OIDC authentication flow. When OIDC authentication is configured, identity tokens submitted during login are accepted without verifying their cryptographic signature. In a vulnerable configuration, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can submit a forged token containing arbitrary identity claims to obtain a fully authenticated technician session. In some configurations, this may also allow bypass of multi-factor authentication.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 1% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 10.0.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-07-02)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached.

impact.affected_versions: SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.

items.title: Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability
items.description: Cisco IOS 12.4 contains multiple cross-site forgery vulnerabilities that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via (1) a certain "show privilege" command to the /level/15/exec/- URI, and (2) a certain "alias exec" command to the /level/15/exec/-/configure/http URI.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 24% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 4.3.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for Cisco IOS per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-07-16)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical.

impact.affected_versions: Cisco IOS (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Check MDM inventory for the named apps; review any linked accounts or API keys for exposure.
